title: Measuring competitiveness in Europe: resource allocation, granularity and trade
summary_en: Co-edited Bruegel Blueprint XXIV synthesising the CompNet research network's findings on European competitiveness. Brings together micro-aggregated firm-level evidence on productivity dispersion, resource misallocation, granularity (big-firm dominance), and trade. Hungary appears throughout as one of the most-analysed CEE benchmarks.
policy_relevance: Frame document for the European Commission and ECB on competitiveness measurement: a single TFP number per country is not enough; misallocation and granularity matter. Hungarian policymakers (MNB, MGFM, NKFI) can use the framework to interpret why headline productivity gaps with the EU15 are not closing. Caveat: large multi-author synthesis; specific channels for Hungary need detail beyond the headline measures.
